title: Fix the Maximum Consecutive Ones Finder

statement.markdown: You are given a binary array nums where `nums[i]` contains 1 or 0. Your task is to find the maximum length of a contiguous subarray with all 1s. You can assume that the input array is not empty.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a binary array of integers (0s and 1s) with a length between 1 and 10,000.
statement.outputFormat: The maximum length of a contiguous subarray with all 1s.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input array is not empty. The array can contain zero or more 1s.

examples.explanation: Explanation: The maximum length of a contiguous subarray with all 1s is 3, which is [1,1,1].
Explanation: The maximum length of a contiguous subarray with all 1s is 2, which is [1,1] or [0,1].

hints.text: Use a sliding window approach to find the maximum length of a contiguous subarray with all 1s.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to maintain a sliding window of consecutive 1s and update the maximum length whenever a new maximum is found.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve iterating over the array and for each subarray with all 1s, calculating its length and updating the maximum length.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using a variable to keep track of the current window length and updating it whenever a new 1 is encountered.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a simple iteration over the array, maintaining a variable to keep track of the current window length and updating it whenever a new 1 is encountered.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that the sliding window approach correctly identifies the maximum length of a contiguous subarray with all 1s.
editorial.dryRun: Here's a step-by-step example run of the optimal solution: Input: [1,1,0,1,1,1]. Current window length: 1. Update window length: 2. Continue iterating over the array and updating the window length whenever a new 1 is encountered.
commonMistakes: Not initializing the window length variable
Not updating the window length whenever a new 1 is encountered
